intro: Ixodia angusta, also known as Ixodia, is a flowering plant species native to Australia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. Ixodia angusta is valued for its traditional use in Australian Aboriginal medicine, where it has been utilized to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ixodia angusta include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ixodia angusta is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
